title: Insert at the Head
prompt: Implement insert_head so it allocates a new node, points it at the current front of the list, and makes it the new front. It needs to modify the caller's head pointer itself, so it takes a pointer to the pointer: struct node **head. With the calls already in main (insert 30, then 20, then 10, each at the head), the final printed list should read 10, 20, 30.
